author: @bill_4615
text:
TWpT: Garbage.
Foundation: Trash.
The Witcher: Feces.

These should be lay-up productions.  They have built in audiences and all you have to do is translate prose into a script.  

They should have stayed true to the source material.  They still may not have been successful, but they would have at least kept, in theory, the bulk of the original fans.

They pick these properties because they know there is an assured, initial, fan base.  It is almost sociopathic that the writers of these shows feel obligated to destroy someone else's work.

I would never take a job and intentionally do something that my employer (the audience) didn't want.  I would never take someone's money and then destroy what they hired me to help make.
